title: Computational Design Platform for CRISPR Epigenome-Editing Therapy in Maternal UPD15 Prader-Willi Syndrome

novel_contributions.description: Integrated 464 significant gRNA sites with CpG islands, hypothalamic ATAC, Capture-C loops, and methylation on unified hg38 coordinates

novel_contributions.contribution: Editor-stratified forward model with honest uncertainty
novel_contributions.description: Rules-informed scoring per mechanism class (Tet1 demethylation vs VP64 activation); cross-editor ML explicitly rejected (CV R² < 0)
PageRank diffusion from gRNA targets to PWS genes through Capture-C regulatory graph
Uncertainty-aware w_tet1/w_vp64 recommendation for validated G4363+G9414 pair
Tet1 ICR demethylation (SNHG14 rescue) + minimal VP64 (SNRPN gap) achieves dual-gene paternal-equivalent window
novel_contributions.evidence: SNRPN 70% WT, SNHG14 98% WT at w_vp64~0.06

novel_contributions.description: AgRP/POMC/OXT dynamics under predicted gene rescue — links molecular design to hyperphagia proxy
novel_contributions.evidence: Hybrid catalog_rank_1 hyperphagia_proxy=0.842 vs Tet1 monotherapy=0.759 — dual-gene window improves circuit normalization

novel_contributions.description: 2520 human epigenome-editing records inform mechanism-class confidence
novel_contributions.evidence: Tet1 demethylation prior: 62% of CRISPRepi Tet1 records report demethylation. VP64 activation prior: 60% report transcriptional effects. These inform mechanism-class confidence in forward model, not locus-specific outcomes.

novel_contributions.description: Held-out guide benchmark + organoid scRNA + neuron RNA (GSE285305) + bisulfite methylation
Full hg38 SpCas9 NGG scan (mm0–mm4) with genomic coordinates; integrated into catalog safety ranking
Brain/embryo DNase, H3K27ac, H3K4me3, CTCF, TFBS at PWS locus via UCSC REST API
Hypothalamic neuron ATAC signal quantified at PWS-ICR from 14 bigWig replicates
Illumina manifest + hg38 liftOver maps GSE28525/GSE298378 probes to PWS imprinted DMRs
Legacy seed-scan counts retained for backward compatibility
Monte Carlo probability that top catalog designs achieve the dual-gene 70–130% WT window under model uncertainty

key_scientific_findings: Tet1 at PWS-ICR demethylates and restores SNHG14 (~100% WT) but under-restores SNRPN (~52% WT)
VP64 at SNRPN promoter hyperactivates SNRPN (~246% WT) without SNHG14 rescue
Hybrid Tet1 + minimal VP64 achieves dual-gene paternal-equivalent window (70-130% WT)
Organoid validation confirms massive SNRPN/SNHG14 reactivation in hypothalamic tissue
Validated guides (G4363, G4670, G9414) recover in top decile within editor context
honest_limitations: Forward model uses editor-level bulk RNA priors, not guide-resolved expression
Hybrid dose-response is phenomenological; requires experimental titration
Cas-OFFinder-style genome scan is in-silico; biochemical validation (CHANGE-seq/GUIDE-seq) still required
Held-out benchmark is small (n=4 validated guides)
Graph propagation and ODE circuit models are hypothesis-generating
Cas-OFFinder-style scan is in-silico; CHANGE-seq/GUIDE-seq validation still required
CNN/GNN deep learning not viable with n=57 Tet1 training guides
what_is_not_novel: Demonstrating PWS gene reactivation via Tet1 (Nemoto 2025, Rohm 2025)
recommended_publication_framing: First computational design-prioritization platform that integrates PWS-locus imprinting biology, multi-omic digital twin, editor-stratified forward models, Pareto/Bayesian optimization, and circuit-level hypothesis generation to rank uncertainty-aware epigenome-editing therapies for maternal UPD15 PWS.
experimental_next_step: PWS UPD15 iPSC -> hypothalamic neuron/organoid differentiation
Test top 3 hybrid designs + Tet1 monotherapy comparator (PWS_G.4363)
Collateral panel: UBE3A, ATP10A, GABRB3 expression; genome-wide methylation
Compare measured outcomes to forward model predictions; iterate model
